for_c_level: This run shows low risk with all 34 executed scenarios passing, supporting production readiness for TLS-off federated deployments. Customer-facing claims of dependable agent memory sharing hold strong. No changes or regressions noted versus prior patch2 runs, maintaining stability.
for_non_technical: AI agents in this test reliably shared memories with each other, ensuring that information written by one was accurately seen by others. There were no issues with losing or mixing up memories in the tests that ran. One test was skipped because of a problem with its report, but everything else worked as expected.
for_sme: All tested primitives passed without failures, covering S1 (basic insert/recall), S10 (delete propagation), S11 (linking), S5 (consolidation), S40 (bulk), and others across federation. Scenario 23 was skipped due to unparseable JSON report, likely a harness flaw (F# report-parsing). No root causes for failures as none occurred; cluster consistency held in 4-node mesh.
headline: Hermes v0.6.2 passes all executed scenarios in TLS-off federation test.
next_run_change: Resolve unparseable report issue for scenario 23 to achieve full coverage in the next campaign.
what_it_proved: Demonstrated reliable propagation and recall of memories across agents with no data loss in 34/35 requested scenarios under TLS-off conditions.
what_it_tested: Tested 34 scenarios exercising memory sharing, deletion, linking, consolidation, pubsub, bulk operations, and recovery across HTTP transport in a 4-node federated mesh framework using core API primitives.
